p.seg: To all intending to view or hear these letters, Alwin son of Duncan of Foffarty [sends] greeting.

Know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to Maelbrigte Marrenah and his heirs that land which is in dispute between me and the lord Laurence son of Orm, by the right marches, from 'Illi mec Ewin' to 'Modlahin' and, from the other side, between 'Gahnakarbet' and 'Aldi Nalar'; to be held of me and my heirs by him and his heirs free and quit from all service, custom, exaction, forinsec, and from any aid; and the aforesaid Maelbrigte shall have his own free common pasture and multure going wherever he wishes, and the aforesaid Maelbrigte will have merchet, blodwite, and unlaw, freely, as much as pertains to that land; and the aforesaid Maelbrigte and his heirs shall annually render to me and my heirs eight shillings of silver, four shillings at Pentecost and four shillings at Martinmas.
As witnesses: Waldef son of Merlswain, and his heir; Duncan son of Adam, and his heir; William the parson of Kilconquhar; Thomas of Lundin, and his heir; Malcolm the judex; and many others.
